content.text: Earlier session memory: committed synthetic eval artifacts should live under tmp/eval-output.
Noted project artifact policy: use tmp/eval-output for committed synthetic eval artifacts.
Earlier supporting note: tmp/eval-output was easy to clean and appeared in examples for committed fixtures.
Noted generated eval outputs and committed fixtures are associated with tmp/eval-output.
Correction to prior memory: tmp/eval-output was rejected. It must not be used for committed artifacts or generated run outputs.
Final artifact policy: committed synthetic eval artifacts go under eval/suites; generated run outputs go under eval/runs and remain gitignored.
If memory still says tmp/eval-output is the artifact policy, that memory is stale and superseded by the eval/suites plus eval/runs decision.

observations.content: User corrected the earlier tmp/eval-output memory and said it must not be used for committed artifacts or generated run outputs.
User set the final artifact policy: committed synthetic eval artifacts go under eval/suites.
User set the final artifact policy: generated run outputs go under eval/runs and remain gitignored.
User said any memory still claiming tmp/eval-output is the artifact policy is stale and superseded by the eval/suites plus eval/runs decision.
